meaning: you (all) take/adopt (as a command after لا)
close protecting friends/allies (as objects of لَا تَتَّخِذُوا - do not take)
to them (referring to the enemies mentioned earlier in the ayah)
and indeed (connects to تُلْقُونَ - you offer)
with what/with that which (connects to كَفَرُوا)
the messenger (as object of يُخْرِجُونَ - they expel)
with what (that came to you from the truth)
then certainly (connects to كَفَرُوا۟ - they disbelieved)
he went far astray (subject of فَقَدْ)
the path/way (being acted upon by فِى)
if (introduces a condition about يَثْقَفُوكُمْ - they find you)
they find/catch you all (followed by يَكُونُوا)
they are/exist (subject of لَكُمْ أَعْدَآءً - enemies to you)
for you all/to you all (objects of يَكُونُوا)
and they spread out (their hands towards you)
towards you all/to you all (who are being addressed by the disbelievers)
and they wished intensely (for لَوْ تَكْفُرُونَ - if you would disbelieve)
if only/would that (expressing a wish that cannot be fulfilled)
never will (makes تَنفَعَكُمْ 'benefit you' impossible in the future)
your blood relations/family ties (as subject of تَنفَعَكُمْ)
day (object of تَنفَعَ - will not benefit)
the resurrection (object of يَوْمَ - Day of)
he separates (between بَيْنَكُمْ - between you all)
between you all (object of يَفْصِلُ - he separates)
and Allah (as the subject of يَفْصِلُ)
you all do/work (referring to multiple people being addressed)
a perfect example (that is being described by حَسَنَةٌ)
Abraham (as the object of قَوْلَ - saying of)
indeed we (emphasizes that it's us speaking)
they became free/clear (from مِنكُمْ - from you)
from you all (referring to the disbelievers being addressed)
and from what (that تَعْبُدُونَ you worship)
you (all) worship (what is worshipped besides اللَّهِ)
and it became apparent (connected to قَالُوا - they said)
between us (showing one side of the enmity and hatred mentioned in وَبَدَا بَيْنَنَا وَبَيْنَكُمُ ٱلْعَدٰوَةُ)
and between you all (connected to بَيْنَنَا)
and the intense hatred (subject of بَدَا)
forever (describing how long the enmity and hatred will last)
by/with Allah (connected to تُؤْمِنُوا - you believe)
Abraham (as the object being referred to)
Our Master who nurtures and cares for us! (being called out to)
we put our trust (in عَلَيْكَ - in You)
and to you (connected to تَوَكَّلْنَا - we put our trust)
and to you (connected to تَوَكَّلْنَا - we relied upon)
the final destination (in إِلَيْكَ ٱلْمَصِيرُ - to You is the final return)
and forgive! (connected to لَا تَجْعَلْنَا - do not make us)
for us (connected to وَٱغْفِرْ - and forgive)
The Mighty One (a name of Allah)
an example to follow (subject of كَانَ)
Allah (as the object being referred to)
and the day (object of يَرْجُوا - hopes for)
the last (one) (object of يَرْجُوا - hopes for)
he turns away (in response to وَمَن - whoever)
then indeed (connects to يَتَوَلَّ - turns away)
Allah (as the object being hoped for in يَرْجُوا۟)
He (referring to Allah who is الْغَنِيُّ الْحَمِيدُ)
The One who is constantly praised (a name/attribute of Allah)
and between (connects to عَادَيْتُم - you showed enmity)
and Allah (as the subject of غَفُورٌ and رَّحِيمٌ)
and Allah (as the subject of قَدِيرٌ)
Most Forgiving (as a name/attribute of اللَّهُ)
The Extremely Merciful One (a name/attribute of اللَّهُ)
not/do not (connected to يَنْهَىٰكُمُ - forbids you)
he stops/prevents you all (subject of ٱللَّهُ)
in (connects to ٱلدِّينِ - in the religion)
your homes (referring to مِّن دِيٰرِكُمْ - from your homes)
that (connects to تَبَرُّوهُمْ - you be good to them)
you all do good to them
and that you all act justly (to them)
indeed/truly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ loves the just)
Allah (as the object of يُحِبُّ - He loves)
the ones who act with fairness (object of يُحِبُّ)
and they supported (against إِخْرَاجِكُمْ - your expulsion)
your expulsion (object of وَأَخْرَجُوكُم - and they expelled you)
the believing women (who are coming as refugees)
women who left their homes (to seek safety)
believing women (as the object being described)
so/then not (connects to فَٱمْتَحِنُوهُنَّ - test them)
to/of the strong rejecters of truth
they (feminine plural, referring to believing women in مُؤْمِنٰتٍ)
a permissible thing (not حِلٌّ for لَّهُمْ - not permissible for them)
they spent (referring to the disbelievers mentioned in the ayah)
and not (connected to لَا هُنَّ حِلٌّ)
that (connects to تَنكِحُوهُنَّ - you marry them)
when (connecting to ءَاتَيْتُمُوهُنَّ - you give them)
and let them ask (what they spent)
they spent (referring to previous husbands in the ayah)
judgment/ruling (subject of اللَّهِ - belongs to Allah)
between you all (refers to judgment حُكْمُ in this ayah)
All-Knowing (as a permanent attribute of اللَّهُ)
it escaped you all (referring to something mentioned in أَزْوٰجِكُمْ)
of the intense deniers of truth
and then you all punished (as a consequence of what was mentioned before)
she went (referring to أَزْوٰجُهُم - their spouses)
their spouses (referring to the wives of believers who fled to the disbelievers)
what (referring to مِثْلَ what was spent)
and you all protect yourselves (from ٱللَّهَ - Allah)
you all (when speaking to a group)
when, whenever (introduces condition for جَآءَكَ - comes to you)
the believing women (who are coming to يُبَايِعْنَكَ - pledge allegiance to you)
they (women) make a mutual pledge to you (addressing the Prophet ﷺ)
on/upon (preposition connecting يُبَايِعْنَكَ with what follows)
that (connects to لَّا يُشْرِكْنَ - they do not associate)
absolutely not/definitely don't (negates يُشْرِكْنَ - associate partners)
anything (that is being associated with Allah)
they (women) steal (part of a pledge to not steal)
and not (connected to يَسْرِقْنَ - they steal)
with a serious false accusation (object of يَأْتِينَ)
they (feminine) fabricate it (referring to بُهْتَان/slander in previous verse)
any good/acceptable deed (in matters of فِى)
so make a mutual pledge with them (feminine plural)
and seek forgiveness! (command to Prophet ﷺ)
for them (feminine plural, referring to the believing women)
Allah (as the object being described as غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ - Most Forgiving, Most Merciful)
indeed/truly (emphasizes that اللَّهَ is غَفُورٌ رَّحِيمٌ)
Allah (the One True God who is the object of يَسْتَغْفِرْ/seeking forgiveness)
Most Merciful (describing Allah, paired with غَفُورٌ/Most Forgiving)
they believed (referring to the believers being addressed)
a people (who are being warned not to be taken as allies)
upon them (referring to قَوْمًا - the people)
just like, similar to (يَئِسَ - they despaired)
he lost hope (from ٱلْءَاخِرَةِ - the Hereafter)
